truth.papers.A77.title: Lightweight Fe–Mn–Al–C Steels: Current State, Manufacturing, and Implementation Prospects
truth.papers.A77.paper_scope: A review of the effects of alloying elements on the structure and properties of austenitic Fe–Mn–Al–C lightweight steels, the formation and distribution of κ-carbides, manufacturing processes, strengthening methods, and challenges for industrial implementation.

prediction.papers.A77.title: Lightweight Fe–Mn–Al–C Steels: Current State, Manufacturing, and Implementation Prospects
prediction.papers.A77.paper_scope: Comprehensive review of lightweight Fe-Mn-Al-C steels covering alloying effects on microstructure and properties, kappa-carbide formation and distribution, manufacturing processes, strengthening mechanisms, and industrial implementation challenges.

truth.alloys.alloy_fe30mn9al1c_mosi.alloys_not: Si range 0.6-0.96 wt%, Mo range 0.5-0.54 wt%; nominal values used.

prediction.alloys.alloy_fe27mn8al1c_v.alloys_notes: V content reported as microalloying; specific wt% not explicitly stated for the Fe-27Mn-8Al-1.0C base in the cited study within this review.

truth.processes.proc_centrifugal_cast.descript: Centrifugal casting, no subsequent heat treatment reported.
truth.processes.proc_anneal_600.processes_note: Annealing time not explicitly stated for this specific alloy; general practice mentions 3 h.
truth.processes.proc_heat_treatment_si.descrip: Heat treatment leading to precipitation of intragranular κ-carbides; includes solutionizing and aging.
truth.processes.proc_heat_treatment_si.process: Exact temperature and time not provided; yield strength reaches ≈950 MPa after treatment.
truth.processes.proc_hm_quench_age.description: Arc melting, casting into ingot, hot rolling at 1200 °C to 10.4 mm thickness, water quenching, aging at 530 °C for 10 h.
truth.processes.proc_hm_quench_age.processes_n: Also reported rolled plate directly aged without quenching (hardness 420 HB after aging).

prediction.processes.proc_centrifugal_cast.descript: Centrifugal casting; as-cast condition with varying carbon content.
prediction.processes.proc_centrifugal_cast.processe: Investigated for C levels of 0.8-1.4 wt%; microstructure and mechanical properties reported for as-cast strips.
prediction.processes.proc_hm_quench_age.description: Arc melting, casting into ingots, hot rolling, and aging.
prediction.processes.proc_hm_quench_age.processes_n: Hot rolling conducted at 1200°C; aging performed at approximately 530°C for 10 h. Armor plate variant also quenched in water prior to aging in some conditions.
prediction.processes.proc_anneal_600.processes_note: Referenced annealing range 500-700°C for 3 h in general study; specific tensile data reported for 600°C annealed 3Cu strips.
prediction.processes.proc_fe20mn11al18c5cr_01.descr: Conventional processing with solution treatment; exact parameters not detailed in review text.
prediction.processes.proc_fe20mn11al18c5cr_01.proce: As reported by Sutou et al.; mechanical properties provided without full process step enumeration.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn132al16c5cr_01.desc: Optimized hot rolling and heat treatment to obtain homogeneous austenitic microstructure.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn132al16c5cr_01.proc: Designed to suppress ferrite formation despite high Al content; Cr added to suppress κ-carbide precipitation.
prediction.processes.proc_fe27mn8al1c_01.descriptio: Thermomechanical processing followed by aging at 450-550°C.
prediction.processes.proc_fe27mn8al1c_01.processes_: Dual precipitation of intragraninal VC/V4C3 and κ-carbides achieved through controlled TMP and aging.
prediction.processes.proc_fe28mn10al1c_01.descripti: Cold rolling followed by solution treatment at 1050°C.
prediction.processes.proc_fe28mn10al1c_01.processes: NbC precipitation and significant grain refinement observed after solution treatment.
prediction.processes.proc_fe21mn9al1c8ni_01.process: Processed via hot rolling to precipitate uniform intragranular B2 NiAl particles.
prediction.processes.proc_heat_treatment_si.descrip: Thermal treatment / aging with varying Si content.
prediction.processes.proc_heat_treatment_si.process: Heat treatment performed on homogenized Fe-30Mn-9Al-1.2C steel with 0, 1, and 2 mass% Si to study κ-carbide evolution.

truth.processing_steps.sample_fe20mn9al3cr_08c: As-cast strips; no further processing mentioned.
truth.processing_steps.sample_fe20mn9al3cr_14c: As-cast strips; no further processing mentioned.
truth.processing_steps.sample_fe28mn9al08c_3cu: Preceded by casting and possibly rolling; annealing at 500–700 °C for 3 h is mentioned for similar Cu-containing alloys.
truth.processing_steps.sample_fe30mn9al12c_2si: Heat treatment promoting intragranular κ-carbides (size 11.5 nm, volume fraction 25.9%); intergranular κ-carbides (50–200 nm) also present.
truth.processing_steps.sample_fe30mn9al1c_mosi: Hardness 360 HB for quenched + aged plate; rolled + aged (without quench) reached 420 HB.

prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe20mn9al3cr_08c: As-cast strip; Figure 6 shows SEM-EBSD phase maps for this composition series.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe20mn9al3cr_14c: As-cast strip with 1.4 wt% C; highest κ-carbide precipitation among the reported series.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe30mn9al1c_mosi: Aging time 10 h; plate hardness reaches 340 HB. Alternative condition at 538°C reported as 360 HB (heat-treated) and 420 HB (rolled + aged).
prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe28mn9al08c_3cu: General study used 500-700°C for 3 h; tensile data quoted specifically for 600°C annealed 3Cu strips.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe27mn8al1c_v.pr: Includes cold rolling (20%) and high-temperature annealing (900°C) followed by aging (450-550°C) as reported for related V-microalloyed systems.
Solution treatment at 1050°C produces fine austenitic grains and NbC precipitation.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe21mn9al1c8ni.p: Hot rolling to precipitate uniform intragranular B2 NiAl particles.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe30mn9al12c_2si: Thermally treated state after homogenization; specific temperature and time not explicitly stated for the 2Si sample in the reviewed text.

truth.interfaces.idx_0.phase_evolution: κ-carbides nucleate by spinodal decomposition or classical nucleation, remain coherent with austenite, low elastic strain facilitates nanoscale precipitation.

truth.properties.sample_fe30mn9al12c_2si_ht.me: increased strain hardening due to localized coarse slip bands; 83% at 2% Si %

prediction.properties.sample_fe30mn9al1c_mosi_age.p: approximately 13% lower than rolled homogeneous armor (RHA)

truth.characterization_methods.sample_fe20mn9a: Ferrite volume fraction 36 vol%, austenite matrix; κ-carbides present
Ferrite volume fraction reduced to 4 vol%, austenite dominant; dense κ-carbides
truth.characterization_methods.sample_fe28mn9a: Cu-rich clusters coprecipitated with κ-carbides after annealing

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe20mn9a: Phase mapping of ferrite and austenite distribution across the C-content series (0.8C-1.4C)

Ferrite (red contrast) and austenite (gray contrast) phase maps reveal ferrite volume fraction decreases from 36 vol% at 0.8C to 4 vol% at 1.4C; DO3 precipitation in ferrite decreases and κ-carbide precipitation in austenite increases with rising C content.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe28mn9a: Elemental microanalysis and Cu distribution in as-cast and annealed 3Cu strips

As-cast and annealed conditions at 500-700°C show evolution of Cu-rich areas; with increasing annealing temperature, large Cu-rich areas decrease while small Cu-rich areas in austenite increase, correlating with κ-carbide precipitation.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Used to calculate phase diagrams of Fe–20Mn–Al–C alloys at 900 °C and 1200 °C (Figure 3)

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Figure 1: Number of publications by research topic (lightweight steel and Fe–Mn–Al–C steel) from 2010 to 2022 according to Scopus bibliometric database.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Figure 2: Flowchart comparing multiphase (Mn: 5-12 wt%, C ≤0.8 wt%) and austenitic (Mn >12 wt%, C 0.8-1.2 wt%) Fe–Mn–Al–C steels; multiphase strength 800-1200 MPa, elongation 30-60%; austenitic strength 600-1500 MPa, elongation <60%.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Figure 4: Mechanical properties of lightweight steels with average and high Mn content at room temperature (reproduced from ref.[8]).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: General classification: Fe–Mn–Al–C steels are divided into ferritic, duplex, and austenitic based on Mn and C content. Austenitic steels with 12–30 wt% Mn, 5–12 wt% Al, 0.6–1.2 wt% C exhibit TWIP/TRIP/MBIP mechanisms.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: SFE ranges: <12 mJ/m² → TRIP; 12–35 mJ/m² → TWIP; 60–100 mJ/m² → MBIP. Al increases SFE, promoting slip and microband-induced plasticity.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: κ-carbide precipitation: intragranular κ’-carbides form by spinodal decomposition (coherent, 2–3 nm), intergranular κ-carbides form by eutectoid reaction at grain boundaries, detrimental to ductility.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Table 1 summarizes the effects of alloying and microalloying elements (Si, Cr, Cu, V, Nb, Mo, Ni) on κ-carbide behavior, microstructure, and mechanical properties.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Additive manufacturing: Laser powder bed fusion (LPBF) of Fe–Mn–Al–C alloys yields tensile strength >900 MPa and elongation >20%, suitable for complex shaping.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Casting challenges: high Mn and Al cause refractory reactions, alumina clogging, and surface defects; new casting schemes needed.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Hydrogen embrittlement: Al additions mitigate hydrogen embrittlement in austenitic Fe–Mn–C steels.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Figure 1 (publication trends 2010-2022), Figure 3 (Fe-20Mn-Al-C phase diagrams at 900°C and 1200°C), and Figure 4 (mechanical properties versus Mn content) are referenced, but detailed numerical values depend on graphical curves not available in the text pipeline.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Stacking fault energy (SFE) ranges for deformation mechanisms are discussed as general thresholds: TRIP below 12 mJ·m⁻², TWIP at 12-35 mJ·m⁻², and MBIP/slip at 60-100 mJ·m⁻², applicable across multiple alloy systems rather than a single sample.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: For Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-0.5Mo-0.9Si armor steel, a rolled-and-aged plate (538°C, 10 h) reached 420 HB, while a heat-treated (quenched + aged) plate reached approximately 360 HB under similar aging.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Addition of 1.0 mass% V to Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.9C steel reportedly achieved tensile strength of 1300 MPa and specific strength of 190 MPa·cm³·g⁻¹ after cold rolling (20%), annealing at 900°C, and aging at 450-550°C.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Aluminum additions (1-2 wt%) enhance resistance to hydrogen-induced embrittlement in high-Mn TWIP steels by increasing SFE and reducing hydrogen diffusivity.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Manufacturing challenges highlighted include phosphorus sensitivity, melt-refractory reactions, alumina nozzle clogging during continuous casting, and red scale defects when silicon exceeds 1-2 mass%.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Aging of Fe-28Mn-9Al-1C steel with 10-12 wt% Al at approximately 550°C can yield approximately 1.15 GPa with approximately 50% elongation due to early formation of coherent nanoscale κ-carbides.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Combined Ti-Mo-Nb addition in Fe-26Mn-8Al-1.5C steel increases yield strength via grain refinement and fine (Ti,Mo,Nb)C particles, but reduces ductility due to coarse κ-carbides and intergranular carbides.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Fe-30Mn-9Al-1.2C steel with 2 mass% Si exhibited strain hardening behavior increasing to 83% (relative measure from 20% at 0Si), but this metric is not a standard strain-hardening rate and is retained here as qualitative context.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: The review notes that lightweight Fe-Mn-Al-C steels are approximately 18% lighter than conventional steels, with reported property ranges of 400-1000 MPa yield strength, 600-2000 MPa ultimate tensile strength, and 30-100% total elongation across the alloy class.
